Name: History of car industry
1Q: What is used to power the first type of vehicles?
tip1: A French inventor, Nicolas-Joseph Cugnot, built the first working self-propelled land-based mechanical vehicle.
2Q: What kind of engines are Diesel and Otto?
tip2: An engine which generates motive power by the burning of petrol, oil, or other fuel with air inside the combustion chamber.
3Q: Who produced the first internal combustion engine in 1867?
tip3: Karl Friedrich Benz also patented the speed regulation system, the spark plug, the carburetor, the clutch, the gear shift, and the water radiator.
4Q: Which car was the first to come off the assembly line
tip4: Thanks to the production system Ford T was the first automobile that many middle-class peoples could afford.
5Q: Where was the first traffic light in the world set in 1914?
tip5: The first traffic light was installed on the corner of East 105th Street and Euclid Avenue in Cleveland, Ohio.
6Q: Which nameplate is the best-selling model in history by 1997?
tip6: Toyota has launched more than ten generations of the world wide popular Corolla.
7Q: What is the first hybrid car to be mass-produced for the world market?
tip7: The Prius is sold in over 90 markets.
8Q: Who is on the Forbes list the first billionaire in the world of automotive sports?
tip8: Michael Schumacher is one of the greatest Formula One drivers best known for racing on the Scuderia Ferrari team.
9Q: What is the first production car with more than 1000hp?
tip9: The SuperSport version of the Veyron is recognized by Guinness World Records as the fastest street-legal production car in the world.
10Q: Where Rimac Automobili began to develop the most advanced electric-powered hypercars and components for other manufacturers?
tip10: Rimac Automobili from Sveta Nedelja, Croatia cooperate with Porsche, Koenigsegg, Aston Martin, Jaguar, Pininfarina, Cupra, etc.
